behaviors.description: Required patient fields are validated before downstream steps; error lists all missing fields.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is validator-to-orchestrator handoff: missing fields must both be aggregated and block downstream calls. Unit-only checks can miss accidental downstream execution. No real OS/network/LLM dependency, so not System/Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake orchestration with missing `name` and `insurance_id` using mocked downstream services; assert response lists both missing fields and insurance/triage/scheduling are not invoked.

behaviors.description: DOB validation rejects future dates, >150 years old, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: This is pure validation/parsing logic within one function/object boundary. No module interaction is required to expose the defect.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven validator test for valid DOB, future date, 151-years-ago date, and malformed strings.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility sends correct patient ID/plan code to payer API and interprets `eligible`/`not eligible`/`pending`.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is adapter wiring: request mapping + response mapping across service and API client modules. In-process fake endpoint/client can catch payload/status translation bugs. Real external HTTP is not required for this specific failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake payer client/server to assert outbound payload fields and verify intake state mapping for each payer status.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, eligibility becomes `pending` and intake continues.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is error-handling handoff between API client failure and orchestration fallback. Simulated timeout/connection exception in-process is sufficient; real network is not strictly required.
behaviors.test_description: Force payer client to raise timeout/connection errors; assert eligibility is set to `pending` and scheduling/confirmation still execute.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation payload includes reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric.
behaviors.justification: This checks module boundary composition (intake -> triage client payload). Stubbed model endpoint is enough; behavior does not depend on model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke triage service with known symptoms/rubric config and inspect captured outbound model request payload.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage assigns high urgency for emergency symptom combinations.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model decisions (classification quality). Deterministic stubs would hide the failure by always returning expected urgency.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated live triage calls on emergency symptom fixtures (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath) and assert high-urgency rate meets threshold.

behaviors.description: Triage result parser stores a clinician-readable explanation field, not only numeric score.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at parser/storage boundary: explanation can be dropped during transformation/persistence. Pure parser unit tests can miss persistence mapping loss.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative triage response into parser + repository layer and assert persisted record includes non-empty explanation text field.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage explanation cites case-specific symptoms and urgency rationale, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: This is output-quality evaluation of real model text. Stubs cannot verify specificity/rationale quality and will mask true failures.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live triage on multiple symptom sets and score explanation specificity (mentions case symptoms + urgency rationale) against rubric.

behaviors.description: Scheduling picks first available slot matching urgency SLA (high same-day, medium within 48h).
behaviors.justification: Needs scheduling logic plus calendar data access interaction. Local DB-backed integration catches ordering/SLA boundary bugs; no real external infra or LLM needed.
behaviors.test_description: Seed calendar DB with candidate slots, run scheduler for high/medium urgency, assert selected slot is earliest within each SLA window.

behaviors.description: Concurrent intakes do not double-book the same time slot.
behaviors.justification: Race condition manifests across scheduler + shared calendar DB transaction/locking behavior. Requires concurrent module interaction with real local DB semantics; not external-system dependent.
behaviors.test_description: Run two parallel scheduling transactions targeting one slot in test DB; assert exactly one booking succeeds and the other retries/fails cleanly.

behaviors.description: Confirmation message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and visit-type prep instructions.
behaviors.justification: Primarily deterministic template/composition logic. One-function/object tests can catch missing fields or wrong instruction mapping.
behaviors.test_description: Test confirmation formatter with fixture inputs per visit type; assert required tokens and correct prep text are present.

behaviors.description: Pipeline orchestration (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) succeeds with deterministic stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification: Explicitly stubbed triage removes LLM-quality dependency; behavior targets cross-module orchestration/state handoff. No required real external infra in the stated behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end intake in-process with stub triage + fake payer/scheduler dependencies and assert stage order, state transitions, and final confirmation artifact.

behaviors.description: Full live intake pipeline completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Run includes live AI triage call in the execution path; real model behavior can break pipeline completion/parsing. Higher Workflow rejected because only one agentic decision stage is specified, not multiple agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a real intake through production-like environment hitting live payer API and live triage model; assert unattended completion through confirmation and persisted audit trail.
